title: feat(core): add support for passing custom `assistantMessageId`

similarOrDuplicatePRs.title: fix(core): fix message ID mismatch between stream/generate response and memory

summary: Adds support for passing a custom `assistantMessageId` when calling `agent.stream()` or `agent.generate()`, allowing callers to specify the ID for the assistant response message saved to memory. Useful for optimistic UI updates, offline reconciliation, or pre-associating message IDs. The change threads the new parameter from the agent API through prepare-stream, model loop types, and into the core loop where it takes priority over `experimental_generateMessageId` and `generateId`. Includes a test in the loop test utils.
maintainerNotes: Well-written PR from an external contributor with clear description, good code quality, and a test. However, it is very stale (450+ days, no activity since Dec 2025) and has merge conflicts (CONFLICTING state). Missing a changeset. A related PR (#13796) fixing message ID mismatch between stream/generate response and memory was merged in March 2026, but it addresses a different concern (ID consistency bug) rather than this feature (user-supplied custom IDs). The feature itself is still not in the codebase. CI checks are stuck in pending state (likely due to the staleness/conflicts). Several test suites never ran. If the feature is desired, this would need a rebase, changeset, and fresh CI run.
